data.title: Updated: Test issue with special characters and backticks
data.body:
This is a test with backtick: ` and special chars: @#$%^&*() and code: `print('hello')`

🔥🚀✨ 奇奇怪怪的测试内容 ✨🚀🔥

## 特殊字符测试
- 中文测试：你好世界
- 日文测试：こんにちは
- 韩文测试：안녕하세요
- Emoji 测试：😀😎🎉🎈🎁
- 数学符号：∑ ∫ ∞ √ ≠ ≤ ≥
- 箭头：→ ← ↑ ↓ ↔
- 货币符号：€ £ ¥ ₩

## 代码块测试
```python
def weird_function():
    """这是一个奇怪的函数"""
    return "Hello World!"
    
print(weird_function())
```

```javascript
const weird = {
    name: "测试",
    value: 123,
    nested: {
        deep: "深层嵌套"
    }
};
```

## 引用测试
> 这是一段引用
> 多行引用
> 继续引用

## 列表测试
1. 第一项
2. 第二项
   - 子项 1
   - 子项 2
3. 第三项

## 链接测试
[GitHub](https://github.com)
[测试链接](https://example.com)

## 空行测试

上面有空行

下面也有空行

## 特殊格式测试
**粗体文字**
*斜体文字*
~~删除线~~
`行内代码`

## 重复内容测试
测试重复测试重复测试重复测试重复

## 结束标记
🎯 测试完成！🎯

data.body: This is a test issue created by automated tests.

data.title: 🐛 [Bug] 删除功能无法正常使用
data.body:
## 问题描述

删除功能无法正常执行，点击删除后没有任何效果。

## 复现步骤

1. 进入相关页面/功能
2. 尝试使用删除功能
3. 观察到删除操作无响应或未生效

## 预期行为

点击删除后，对应内容应被成功删除。

## 实际行为

删除操作无法完成，内容仍然存在。

## 环境信息

- 请补充操作系统、浏览器版本等相关信息

---
*此 Issue 由 Claude 协助创建*

data.title: [Task] Establish Automated Issue Triage Protocol
data.body:
**Description:**
There is currently no strict triage process for incoming community issues, resulting in feature requests being mixed with critical bugs. We need to implement an automated labeling system and establish a triage protocol.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Create a GitHub Actions workflow for auto-labeling incoming issues (e.g., `bug`, `enhancement`, `security`, `documentation`).
- Draft and publish a triage protocol document detailing the weekly rotating "triage captain" responsibilities.

data.title: [Task] Expand E2E Testing for Multi-Cloud Environments
data.body:
**Description:**
The CI pipeline currently lacks end-to-end (E2E) testing for multi-cloud environments, which increases the risk of regressions when deploying new cloud provider integrations.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Allocate sprint time to build out a robust E2E testing matrix.
- Simulate evidence collection across AWS, Azure, and GCP simultaneously in the test environment.
- Integrate the multi-cloud E2E tests into the standard CI pipeline to block regressions.

data.title: [Documentation] Improve API Documentation Coverage for Reporting Module
data.body:
**Description:**
Several newly added REST endpoints for the reporting module lack comprehensive Swagger/OpenAPI documentation. 

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Add complete OpenAPI/Swagger annotations to all reporting module REST endpoints.
- Verify generated documentation is accurate and reflects actual request/response schemas.
- Ensure documentation is accessible via the standard `/docs` route.

data.title: [Bug/Enhancement] Handle Slack Integration Rate Limiting
data.body:
**Description:**
The alerting system occasionally hits Slack API rate limits during high-volume compliance drift events, causing missed alerts.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Implement exponential backoff and retry logic for the Slack API integration.
- Batch alerts where possible to reduce the total number of API calls during high-volume drift events.
- Add monitoring to log when rate limits are approached.

data.title: [Feature] UI for Custom Framework Mapping
data.body:
**Description:**
Users are requesting a UI feature to map custom internal policies to standard frameworks (e.g., NIST, ISO, SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR). There is currently a gap in frontend development resources for this, but it is a highly requested feature for the reporting dashboards.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Create frontend UI components for custom policy mapping.
- Integrate the UI with existing backend mapping endpoints.
- Update user documentation to include instructions on how to use the custom framework mapping.

data.title: [Security] Fix PII Exposure in Audit Logs (Data Masking)
data.body:
**Description:**
PII (Personally Identifiable Information) is occasionally exposed in debug-level audit logs, requiring immediate patching for GDPR compliance. As a compliance tool, any mishandling of PII undermines the platform's core value proposition.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Audit logging mechanism must mask or redact all PII at the debug level.
- Introduce automated tests to verify PII masking in logs.
- Ensure compliance with GDPR data handling requirements.
- Backport fix to the upcoming v2.3.2 hotfix release.

data.title: [Enhancement] Update Terraform Provider for latest AWS resources
data.body:
**Description:**
The ComplianceHarbor Terraform provider needs to be updated to support the latest AWS resource types to ensure comprehensive infrastructure-as-code (IaC) scanning.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Map and implement support for new AWS resources in the Terraform provider.
- Pass all acceptance tests for the updated provider.
- Update the provider documentation with the newly supported resources.

data.title: [Bug] OPA (Open Policy Agent) Memory Leak during large Kubernetes manifest evaluation
data.body:
**Description:**
High memory consumption has been observed when evaluating large Kubernetes manifests in the policy engine. This memory leak is blocking enterprise users from scanning large-scale Kubernetes clusters.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Identify and resolve the memory leak in the OPA engine during large evaluations.
- Memory consumption should remain stable and within acceptable limits during large-scale K8s scans.
- Add profiling and benchmark tests for memory usage.
- Target fix for the v2.3.2 hotfix release.

data.title: [Tech Debt] Refactor Test Suite from unittest to pytest
data.body:
**Description:**
Migrate legacy unit tests from `unittest` to `pytest` for better maintainability and to leverage advanced testing features like fixtures and parameterization.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Convert all existing `unittest` test cases to `pytest`.
- Ensure the CI pipeline runs `pytest` successfully without any drops in test coverage.
- Remove `unittest` dependencies where applicable.

data.title: [Bug] Authentication Token Expiry Bug in AWS Config Integrations
data.body:
**Description:**
Intermittent failures occur when renewing OAuth tokens for AWS Config integrations. This causes evidence collection jobs to fail silently without notifying the user or system administrators.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Fix the OAuth token renewal logic for AWS Config integrations.
- Ensure evidence collection jobs report failures appropriately and trigger alerts (no silent failures).
- Add robust unit tests for the token renewal and error handling logic.

data.body:
PR 제목: Update: 로그 기능 설명 추가

PR 내용:

### 요약  
이 PR은 ReadMe 파일에 로그 기능에 대한 설명을 추가합니다.

### 이유  
로그 기능에 대한 문서화가 부족하여 사용자가 이 기능을 효과적으로 활용하는 방법에 혼란을 겪고 있었습니다. 이 업데이트는 이를 해결하기 위한 것입니다.

### 변경 사항  
- `ReadMe.md` 파일에 "기능" 섹션 아래에 "로그 기능" 설명을 추가하였습니다.  
- 로그 출력을 확인하는 방법에 대한 간단한 설명을 포함했습니다.

### 테스트  
해당 변경은 문서 업데이트이므로 추가 테스트는 필요하지 않습니다.

### 참고  
문서화 톤과 표현이 전체적인 가이드와 일치하는지 확인 부탁드립니다.

data.title: Add Hello World examples in multiple programming languages
data.body:
## Summary
This PR adds a collection of "Hello World" example programs written in two new programming languages to serve as reference implementations or starter templates.

## Changes
- **helloworld.js** - JavaScript implementation using console.log
- **helloworld.py** - Python implementation using print function

## Details
Each example demonstrates the basic syntax and conventions for printing output in its respective language. The implementations follow standard practices for each language, including appropriate headers, imports, and output formatting.

data.title: fix: ddd (Closes #4099)
data.body:
## Description
This PR fixes issue #4099

## Changes
- 3 file(s) modified

## Analysis
It looks like we have a bit of a mystery on our hands!

Since the issue title is simply "ddd" and the issue body contains only the text "vdvdv", I'm going to take a guess that this might not be a particularly well-documented or specific issue.

However, based on the fact that it's a GitHub issue, my assumption is that someone has created an issue report for a project hosted on GitHub. Without any further information, it's difficult to determine what exactly needs to be fixed.

To provide a clear description of the problem and expected behavior, I'll make some educated guesses:

**Problem:** It seems that there might be a bug or issue with the codebase that someone has encountered. The lack of descriptive text makes it challenging to identify the specific area of concern or the steps leading up to this issue.

**Expected Behavior:** Given the brevity of the issue description, I'll assume that the expected behavior is simply for the reported issue to be investigated and resolved. Ideally...

data.title: Create hello
data.body: The **Fork and Pull Request workflow** on GitHub allows developers to collaborate safely. A contributor first **forks** a repository and clones it locally. They create a new **branch**, make changes, commit them, and push the branch to GitHub. Then they open a **Pull Request** to propose merging their changes into the original project. After review and approval, the changes are **merged** into the main branch.
